repo_prior_art.cite: pid_long_term_surrogate angle0: closed-form binary transport endpoints conditional on exact endpoint projection

published_prior_art.cite: Manski1990/BalkePearl1997: generic finite-support sharp bounds; no surrogate-specific dilation

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_long_term_surrogate angle0: generic noisy-panel drift LP and binary transport endpoint conjecture

published_prior_art.cite: HuangWangYuanZhaoZhang2026 gives longitudinal surrogate/pivot point extrapolation; FanManzanaresParkQi2026 gives surrogate-index identified sets under unknown copulas

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_long_term_surrogate angle0: sign boundary from binary transport endpoints

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The binary dilation theorem is a simplified version of the archived same-qid transport endpoint machinery, with the hard endpoint projection/pasting issue assumed away.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship kernel still reads as generic finite-support transport sharpness after reducing all short-panel content to [ell_d,u_d]; this is below novelty_target=field.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The point-ID phase diagram is algebra from the dilation interval, not a distinct field-tier surrogate-index theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Overall
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal misses closer repair directions already identified in the rewind context: minimal noisy-panel strict-tightening, common-kernel bridge compatibility, or a sharp counterexample.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the prevalence-only dilation kernel with a minimal K=2,J=1 noisy-panel theorem showing when the earlier wave strictly tightens the terminal-only surrogate bound.
Add a common-kernel bridge compatibility statement coupling the experimental measurement kernel to P^B, so the contribution is not separable bridge-minus-drift arithmetic.

If the positive theorem is too strong, pivot to an explicit sharp counterexample resolving the conjecture and proving that the longitudinal/pivot indices can impose no extra restriction beyond [ell_d,u_d].

recommended_next_step: Reject this prevalence-dilation proposal and pivot to one of the field-tier kernels named in the rewind context.
